keyExpressions.en: You must not use your phone in class.
keyExpressions.ko: 수업 중에 핸드폰을 사용하면 안 됩니다.

keyExpressions.en: You must not use the flash.
You must not bring any food inside.
You must not touch the paintings.

exercises.q: You ___ not run in the hallway.
May I sit here? - No, you ___.

exercises.exp: must not은 '~해서는 안 된다'(금지), don't have to가 '~할 필요 없다'

passages.title: 도입 - 누가 모나리자에 케이크를 던졌나?
passages.en: Last Saturday, someone threw a cake at the Monalisa in the Botero Museum in Bogota, Colombia. There were four eyewitnesses. What did they say? Read the following, and find the criminal.
passages.ko: 지난 토요일, 누군가 콜롬비아 보고타의 보테로 박물관에 있는 모나리자에 케이크를 던졌다. 네 명의 목격자가 있었다. 그들은 뭐라고 말했을까? 다음을 읽고 범인을 찾아보자.

passages.title: 목격자 진술
passages.en:
Mrs. Garcia: I was in the souvenir shop. I was looking for the most beautiful postcard for my daughter.
Mr. Lee: I was in the restroom. I was washing my hands.
Mr. Kim: I was on the first floor. I was waiting for my friend.
Ms. Lopez: I was in the garden. I was taking pictures of some flowers.
passages.ko:
가르시아 부인: 저는 기념품점에 있었어요. 딸을 위해 가장 아름다운 엽서를 찾고 있었어요.
이 씨: 저는 화장실에 있었어요. 손을 씻고 있었어요.
김 씨: 저는 1층에 있었어요. 친구를 기다리고 있었어요.
로페즈 씨: 저는 정원에 있었어요. 꽃 사진을 찍고 있었어요.

passages.title: 단서 - Ann Jones (방문객)
passages.en: I was looking at the Monalisa, and someone threw a cake at the painting. I turned around and saw an old man. He was standing in front of a wheelchair. I'm about 170 cm tall, and he was a little taller than me.
passages.ko: 저는 모나리자를 보고 있었는데, 누군가 그림에 케이크를 던졌어요. 뒤를 돌아보니 한 노인이 있었어요. 그는 휠체어 앞에 서 있었어요. 저는 키가 약 170cm인데, 그는 저보다 조금 더 컸어요.

passages.title: 단서 - Carlos Diaz (청소부) & Camila Santos (빵집 주인)
passages.en:
Carlos: An old man with gray hair was running away, and something fell off his head. It was his wig. I ran after him, but I couldn't catch him. He was faster than me. In fact, the old man was not old. He was a young man with long brown hair.
Camila: Last Friday, a young man came in. I spoke to him in Spanish, but he didn't understand me. He spoke only English. He wanted the smallest cake. He had blue eyes.
passages.ko:
카를로스: 회색 머리의 노인이 도망가고 있었는데, 머리에서 무언가 떨어졌어요. 가발이었어요. 뒤쫓았지만 잡을 수 없었어요. 그가 저보다 빨랐거든요. 사실 그 노인은 나이 든 사람이 아니었어요. 긴 갈색 머리의 젊은 남자였어요.
카밀라: 지난 금요일, 젊은 남자가 들어왔어요. 스페인어로 말했지만 이해하지 못했어요. 영어만 했어요. 가장 작은 케이크를 원했어요. 파란 눈이었어요.

exercises.q: He was ___ than me. (키가 더 큰)
I was looking for the ___ beautiful postcard.
She wanted the most small cake.
I was look at the painting.

points.title: 비교급과 최상급
points.explanation: 비교급: 형용사/부사 + -er + than (더 ~한). 최상급: the + 형용사/부사 + -est (가장 ~한). 긴 단어(3음절 이상): more/most + 원급. 규칙: small→smaller→smallest, big→bigger→biggest, heavy→heavier→heaviest, beautiful→more beautiful→most beautiful

examples.en: It was the most beautiful postcard.

points.title: 조동사 must / must not
points.explanation: must: ~해야 한다 (의무). must not: ~해서는 안 된다 (금지). don't have to: ~할 필요 없다 (불필요). must + 동사원형

examples.en: You must not use the flash.

exercises.q: He is ___ than his brother. (키가 큰)
This is the ___ movie I've ever seen. (재미있는)
She is more tall than me.
He is the most fast runner.
